title: fix kebab credential keys in agent-vault-provision (lead-l95x)
[green] rewrite agent-vault-provision GitHub-PAT path against real 0.32.0 surface (ADR-026 D2) + fleet token + .env write
description: Rewrite bin/agent-vault-provision GitHub-PAT leg: owner auth register (first=owner), vault create, vault credential set, vault service add github.com basic over MITM proxy, agent create slug-fleet --token-only, write AGENT_VAULT_ADDR+TOKEN to .env. Preserve Claude-OAuth human-gate. Audit all verbs.
notes:
mechanism_observation: agent-vault provision authored vs installed 0.32.0 --help only; live-broker e2e is the dummyco-spike's job — The lead-beym rewrite of bin/agent-vault-provision was authored against the agent-vault 0.32.0 CLI **syntax** as printed by the installed /usr/local/bin/agent-vault `--help` surfaces — NOT validated end-to-end against a live broker (no broker runs in the BC env). The verbs the rewrite settled on, all confirmed real:

- owner bootstrap: `agent-vault auth register --email <e> --password-stdin` (help: 'The first user to register becomes the instance owner'); `agent-vault auth login --email --password-stdin` on re-run.
- vault: `agent-vault vault create <vault>`.
- credential: `agent-vault vault credential set <key=value> [..] --vault <vault>` (usage: 'set <key=value> [key2=value2 ...]').
- service (github.com Basic over the single MITM proxy :14322): `agent-vault vault service add --name github --host github.com --auth-type basic --username-key github-pat-user --password-key github-pat --vault <vault>` (help lists auth-type 'basic' + --username-key/--password-key).
- fleet token: `agent-vault agent create <slug>-fleet --token-only --vault <vault>:proxy` (help: 'Create a new agent and print its token'; --token-only 'output only the raw agent token'; --vault 'name:role, role defaults to proxy').

Material nuance vs ADR-026 D2's prose summary (does NOT change the flow, but worth pinning for the next BC dispatch): D2 names 'credential set' / 'service add' / 'agent create' generically; on the real 0.32.0 surface credential/service are sub-verbs UNDER `vault` (`vault credential set`, `vault service add`), while the agent token mint is the TOP-LEVEL `agent create` (the `vault agent` group only adds an EXISTING agent to a vault — it does not mint). The owner-account leg is `auth register` (self-signup, first=owner), not an `owner ...` sub-verb. The owner/credential/service/agent flow D2 specifies maps cleanly onto these real verbs.

DELIVERY CURRENCY: this fix is committed but UNRELEASED — it rides the v0.7.0 batch with lead-ld7i; the lead router cuts v0.7.0 + re-pours after both work_done land. No per-bugfix release was cut.
close_reason: Closed

title: BUG: lead-llc1 .env.example template not shipped in package (pyproject templates/ops/* glob excludes dotfile) — installed bootstrap crashes
description: lead-llc1 adds src/shop_templates/templates/ops/.env.example and renders it via _render_lead_env_example. But pyproject.toml [tool.setuptools.package-data] uses glob 'templates/ops/*', which does NOT match leading-dot files. Reviewer blocked work_done for lead-llc1 on this.

title: GREEN: declare scenarios dep + move scenarios import into work-done hash path
description: Add scenarios @ git+https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-scenarios@v0.2.0 to pyproject dependencies (matches shopsystem-messaging convention). Move 'from scenarios.hash import compute_scenario_hash' out of module top-level into check_scenario_hashes (lazy). Tests go GREEN.
notes: mechanism_observation: Packaging-currency guards assert entry-point declaration + module presence but NOT clean-install importability — tmpl-20n (bc-emit dead-on-arrival) escaped to the v0.6.0 distribution despite test_packaging_currency.py carrying bc-emit currency guards. Those guards (test_delivered_artifact_declares_bc_emit_console_script, test_delivered_artifact_ships_bc_emit_module) assert the [project.scripts] declaration is present and the module file rides in the sdist/wheel — but NEVER that the installed console-script actually IMPORTS on a clean install. A module-top-level 'from scenarios.hash import ...' with an undeclared dependency passes both guards yet fails at first invocation in any env that lacks the undeclared dep. This is a generic gap, not specific to bc-emit: any future package-delivered console-script whose module top-level imports an undeclared transitive dependency will escape the same way. The lead-ld7i fix closes the gap for bc-emit (new tests/test_bc_emit_scenarios_dependency.py proves bc-emit --help exits 0 with scenarios absent), but the currency-guard pattern itself remains import-blind. NOTE (delivery currency): this fix does NOT cut a release; it batches with lead-beym into ONE v0.7.0 the lead router cuts after both work_done land. The currency floor (MIN_VERSION) and re-pour must advance for the fix to reach launched BCs.
close_reason: GREEN: pyproject declares scenarios @ git+https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-scenarios@v0.2.0; bc_emit scenarios import moved into check_scenario_hashes. feat(green) b8fc3d8

title: bc-emit console-script dead-on-arrival in launched BCs: imports scenarios.hash at module load but shop-templates declares no scenarios dependency
description: v0.6.0 (lead-bglz) shipped the bc-emit console-script (lead-m56e). src/shop_templates/bc_emit.py:75 does 'from scenarios.hash import compute_scenario_hash' at MODULE TOP-LEVEL, but pyproject [project].dependencies=[] — scenarios is NOT declared. A clean 'pip install shop-templates@v0.6.0' into a launched BC will not pull scenarios, so 'bc-emit' fails at import with ModuleNotFoundError: scenarios. Confirmed empirically during lead-bglz release verification (bc-emit --help in a clean venv). This undermines the lead-bsam prerequisite (bc-emit reaching launched BCs) that lead-bglz's own message names. FIX: declare scenarios in shop-templates dependencies (if installable in the launcher's index) and/or make the bc_emit scenarios import lazy so non-work-done paths don't fail; deliver via a v0.6.x follow-up release. Discovered: 2026-06-12.
close_reason: FIXED by lead-ld7i: (a) scenarios declared VCS-pinned in pyproject [project].dependencies (scenarios @ git+https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-scenarios@v0.2.0, matching shopsystem-messaging convention); (b) scenarios import made lazy inside check_scenario_hashes. Clean-venv proof: bc-emit --help exits 0 with no ModuleNotFoundError. SHAs: test(red) 13a1cde, feat(green) b8fc3d8.

title: wire the generic ops-parity guard in (passing against the intact explicit-entry package-data)
description: GREEN: with the explicit templates/ops/.env.example package-data entry intact (lead-llc1 24bfb89), the generic source-vs-artifact ops parity guard passes — all 7 ops files ship in both sdist and wheel.
notes:
mechanism_observation: lead-llc1 self-resolved its reviewer-block before lead-emui arrived; .env.example fix already on origin/main (24bfb89) — **Lead-model vs reality mismatch.** The lead-emui dispatch was authored expecting lead-llc1's .env.example packaging deliverable to still be BLOCKED (PRE-STATE cites lead-llc1's blocked work_done + BC sub-issue tmpl-263), with lead-emui completing it. Reality on arrival: the BC had already autonomously self-resolved the lead-llc1 reviewer-block (per the work-done-gate 'After Resolving a Block' path), lead-llc1 had ALREADY emitted work_done --status complete, and the explicit templates/ops/.env.example package-data fix + its packaging-currency test were already merged to origin/main as 24bfb89 ('ship templates/ops/.env.example in built package', work_id lead-llc1). lead-llc1's parent bead tmpl-r02 is CLOSED on origin/main (8f848e7).

Consequence for lead-emui: there was NO broken behavior left to fix. lead-emui therefore did NOT re-implement criterion 1 (that would have duplicated, or if it had added the broad templates/ops/.* glob the criterion offers as an alternative, REVERSED lead-llc1's deliberate explicit-entry choice — the package-data comment explicitly rejects templates/ops/.* as a stray-dotfile-sweep hazard). Instead lead-emui (a) re-verified the four criteria end-to-end against freshly built artifacts and a clean-venv wheel install, and (b) added a strictly-additive GENERIC source-vs-artifact ops parity guard that future-proofs the same bug class for the NEXT ops file.

**Why load-bearing:** this reconciles the lead's two-work-item model (lead-llc1 blocked + lead-emui completes it) against the actual single-completion reality (both complete; lead-llc1 self-resolved). The dispatch's own SCOPE note says 'On completion lead-llc1 reconciles+closes' — that reconciliation is already done. Delivery-currency: the .env.example fix + this guard ride the SAME planned v0.6.0 release+re-pour as lead-llc1 (broker half), lead-m56e, and lead-yap9; no separate cut is needed.
close_reason: Closed

title: RED: failing negative scenario 6d95d409 — bc-emit work-done tag mode REFUSES a tag whose lineage does NOT anchor the work_id (tag points at seed commit)

Add negative step def: temp repo with a tag pointing at a non-work_id seed commit, origin/main carries the work_id elsewhere or not at all; invoke bc-emit work-done in tag mode; assert exit non-zero + error names tag-lineage-anchors-work_id precondition + the tag + the work_id + respond NOT invoked. RED against the CURRENT permissive check_tag_reachable (it wrongly accepts -> scenario fails for the right reason).
close_reason: RED landed: test(red) b9b9e78 — negative scenario 6d95d409 pinned + step defs; failed for the right reason (permissive check_tag_reachable accepted wrong-lineage tag, EXIT=0).

title: GREEN: tighten check_tag_reachable to require the tag's lineage to anchor the work_id (git log <tag> --grep=<work_id>)
description: After confirming the tag exists/resolves, run git log <tag> --grep=<work_id> --fixed-strings; if empty, raise PreconditionRefusal naming the tag-lineage-anchors-work_id precondition + tag + work_id + _SELF_RESOLVE. Keeps positive 12c98d2f green (its tag lineage carries the work_id) and all other lead-m56e scenarios green.
close_reason: GREEN landed: feat(green) fb92520 — check_tag_reachable now requires git log <tag> --grep=<work_id> --fixed-strings non-empty; refuses wrong-lineage tag naming tag-lineage-anchors-work_id precondition + tag + work_id.

title: plan: tighten bc-emit work-done tag-mode reachability — tag lineage must anchor the work_id (lead-yap9; closes tmpl-dhn)
description: check_tag_reachable currently treats 'points at the expected commit lineage' as merely 'tag resolves to a commit with non-empty git rev-list' — the work_id is used ONLY in error text, never to verify the tag's lineage carries/anchors the work_id. So a wrong-lineage tag (e.g. v-unrelated pointing at the repo seed commit, no work_id) SATISFIES and the wrapper proceeds to respond (verified EXIT=0 on tmpl-dhn). TIGHTEN: after the tag exists/resolves, verify the tag's commit LINEAGE carries the work_id, mirroring check_commit_reachable's attribution but scoped to the TAG's history (git log <tag> --grep=<work_id> --fixed-strings must be non-empty). If empty -> refuse (exit non-zero, no respond) with a named-cause error naming the tag-lineage-anchors-work_id precondition + the offending tag + the work_id, reusing _SELF_RESOLVE. Adds NEGATIVE scenario 6d95d409dd527be6; keeps positive 12c98d2f7e5259a9 green. Closes tmpl-dhn.
close_reason: lead-yap9 complete. test(red) b9b9e78 -> feat(green) fb92520; merged to main 6a5e317 (literal lead-yap9). Negative 6d95d409 GREEN, positive 12c98d2f stays GREEN, all lead-m56e scenarios green. Zero newly-failing vs d175ed2 (21 pre-existing tmpl-w1w unchanged). Reviewer gate next.

title: scenario gap: 12c98d2f tag-mode reachability does not pin that a wrong-lineage tag must refuse (bc-emit accepts ANY existing tag)
description: Reviewer (lead-m56e) probe: bc-emit work-done tag mode (check_tag_reachable) treats 'expected commit lineage' as merely 'tag resolves to a commit with non-empty rev-list' — work_id is used ONLY in error text, never to verify the tag corresponds to the work_id's lineage. An unrelated tag (e.g. v-unrelated pointing at the repo seed commit, no work_id) SATISFIES the check and the wrapper proceeds to invoke respond (verified: EXIT=0). Scenario 12c98d2f9e... only asserts (a) does-not-refuse-on-origin/main-HEAD-absence and (b) satisfaction-via-tag-not-origin/main; it pins NO negative case (wrong/unexpected-lineage tag must refuse). This is a SPECIFICATION gap, not an implementer fault — clarify emitted to lead to tighten the scenario (e.g. tag commit must carry the work_id / be the expected lineage anchor) before work_done can be signed off.
close_reason: Resolved by lead-yap9: check_tag_reachable tightened to require the tag's lineage to carry/anchor the work_id (git log <tag> --grep=<work_id>), mirroring check_commit_reachable scoped to the tag's history. Negative scenario 6d95d409dd527be6 now pins that a wrong-lineage tag (e.g. tag on the repo seed commit) REFUSES. feat(green) fb92520; merge 6a5e317.

title: RED: write the failing test for bc-emit work-done self-resolve messaging on any precondition refusal (directs BC to fix its OWN bead/commit/tree + re-invoke; never names lead/router/non-BC actor) — hash 4a6133f7b5f061a2
notes:
mechanism_observation: bc-emit work-done codifies work-done-gate prose executably; prose preconditions retire-able + delivery-currency applies — The bc-emit work-done wrapper (src/shop_templates/bc_emit.py, console_scripts entry point bc-emit) lands the EXECUTABLE form of the work-done-gate skill's prose preconditions (clean-tree w/ carve-outs, commit/tag reachability, scenario-hash match via block-only scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash). Two load-bearing consequences for the next BC dispatch:

1. PROSE-RETIREMENT: the work-done-gate SKILL.md and the bc-implementer/bc-reviewer template-prose preconditions now have an executable equivalent. Scenario f81ee56bc163934b deliberately frames --force as INDEPENDENT of the wrapper so 'landing the wrapper and retiring the prose preconditions does not remove the --force escape valve'. The lead may later schedule a maintenance pass to retire/redirect the prose checks (currently re-asserted across multiple template features, part of the pre-existing tmpl-w1w red set) to point at bc-emit instead of re-stating the checks inline. Not done here (primary deliverable was the wrapper + its 6 scenarios); flagged so the lead can decide whether/when.

2. DELIVERY-CURRENCY: bc-emit ships via the installed shop-templates package entry point. It reaches launched BC shops only AFTER a shop-templates version bump + tag (the same separate step noted for lead-faua/lead-r9r5). The wrapper is package-delivered (entry point), not poured into .claude/, so it is available wherever shop-templates is installed.

3. TEST SEAM: 'did NOT invoke shop-msg respond on refusal' is asserted structurally via a --respond-cmd seam pointed at a recorder that logs only when executed (PreconditionRefusal raised before the respond call), not a tautology. --force independence is asserted against shop-msg's own --help surface because the bare primitive lives in the shop-msg package, outside this BC root.
clarify: Scenario gap in 12c98d2f7e5259a9 (TAG-deliverable reachability): the scenario stipulates the tag 'points at the expected commit lineage' in its Given but its Then steps only assert (a) the wrapper does NOT refuse on origin/main-HEAD-absence and (b) satisfaction comes from the tag rather than origin/main reachability. It pins NO negative: a tag pointing at an UNEXPECTED lineage (no relationship to the work_id) is never required to refuse. Adversarial probe confirms the implementation exploits this — check_tag_reachable accepts ANY existing tag whose rev-list is non-empty; work_id is used only in error text, never to verify the tag's lineage. A tag pointing at the repo's unrelated seed commit (carrying no work_id) SATISFIES the check (verified exit 0, respond invoked). Please tighten 12c98d2f to pin that a tag whose commit does NOT carry / anchor the dispatched work_id's expected lineage must REFUSE (exit non-zero, names the tag + work_id, does not invoke respond), so 'expected commit lineage' is operationally checkable. The other 5 scenarios are faithfully realized and the work-done-gate (clean tree, origin/main reachability of d3f385f, block-only scenario-hash integrity, closed bd plan tmpl-btj.1-.6, test(red) 48f55a5 -> feat(green) 5220079) all pass; only 12c98d2f's underspecification blocks sign-off.

title: tmpl: bump shop-templates to 0.6.0 (green)
notes: mechanism_observation: v0.6.0 ships bc-emit console-script that is dead-on-arrival in launched BCs (no scenarios dependency declared) — During lead-bglz (v0.6.0) release verification, a clean `pip install -U shop-templates@v0.6.0` into a throwaway venv installed the `bc-emit` console-script but `bc-emit --help` raised `ModuleNotFoundError: scenarios`. Root cause: `src/shop_templates/bc_emit.py` imports `from scenarios.hash import compute_scenario_hash` at MODULE TOP-LEVEL, but shop-templates `pyproject [project].dependencies = []` does not declare `scenarios`. It only works in this BC's dev env because `scenarios` 0.2.0 happens to be installed there; a launched BC that installs shop-templates@v0.6.0 will NOT pull it. LOAD-BEARING: lead-bglz's own message names bc-emit package-delivery as a prerequisite for lead-bsam — so as shipped, bc-emit is non-functional in exactly the launched-BC target that needs it. The v0.6.0 RELEASE itself is correct (version, tag, ancestry, infisical+.env.example all verified); this is a pre-existing packaging gap in the lead-m56e wrapper that v0.6.0 has now propagated to the distribution. Fix needs a v0.6.x follow-up. Tracked BC-side as tmpl-20n.
close_reason: GREEN: bumped to 0.6.0, 11/11 currency tests pass; integrated 170f9cc

description: Feature + step defs fail (StepDefinitionNotFound) before authoring steps; against pre-lead-faua hardcoded ops the dummyco-scoped assertions (dummyco-postgres/dummyco network/DUMMYCO_DATA/Dockerfile.dummyco-shell/zero-shopsystem) cannot be produced (true RED). Two real gaps vs the lead's exact pin: (1) compose.yaml comment carried literal 'fleet' -> fails 1b6dbe8a0095fb8f dummyco no-fleet; (2) shop-shell grep targeted <slug>-bc- not <slug>-postgres/<slug>-agent-vault -> fails 5335c39eb06f7493 grep clause. Both fixed additively.
notes:
mechanism_observation: 6 lead-r9r5 hashes pin already-delivered ops generification (lead-faua+lead-w87b); 3 required real gap-fixes — These 6 scenarios (8fcf898fdeebc6be, 9c8b8b40ee9ffde4, 5335c39eb06f7493, abe57dcb4d6f6554, cb1e585684ff4a14, 1b6dbe8a0095fb8f) are the lead's slug-parametric re-authoring of the SUPERSEDED 133/134/138 plus the grown ops file-set, now landed in this BC's features/. They PIN behavior already delivered under lead-faua (compose/shop-shell generification, container_name <slug>-postgres/<slug>-agent-vault, networks/volumes all slug-keyed, pgdata from <SLUG_UPPER>_DATA, crc32 host ports 5829/5714 env-overridable via <SLUG_UPPER>_POSTGRES_PORT, broker-wired shop-shell) and lead-w87b (agent-vault-provision/check; the 6-file ops set). Fits-existing-capability: 11/14 Example instances were GREEN immediately against the current render.

Three assertions were a REAL gap between the lead's EXACT pin and the lead-faua/lead-w87b render, fixed minimally+additively in feat(green) 1fc4cd8 (no regression to the shopsystem default or any other pinned hash):
1. 5335c39eb06f7493 grep clause: shop-shell greped only '<slug>-bc-', not the pinned '<slug>-postgres'/'<slug>-agent-vault' CONTAINER NAME. Added a slug-scoped readiness grep ('^<slug>-postgres$|^<slug>-agent-vault$') after 'docker compose up', non-zero exit + stderr diagnostic if neither is up.
2. 5335c39eb06f7493 no-dotfile clause: shop-shell's ZERO-HOST-COUPLING comment literally contained '~/.claude' / '~/.gitconfig' (the pin forbids those + $HOME variants anywhere in the body). Reworded.
3. 1b6dbe8a0095fb8f no-fleet clause: compose.yaml's host-port comment literally contained 'fleet', tripping the dummyco zero-cross-product-literal assertion. Reworded.

Load-bearing for the lead: the lead can now keep 133/134/138's intent pinned slug-parametrically; the gap-fixes mean a freshly bootstrapped non-default product (dummyco) renders ops/ with zero 'fleet'/'shopsystem' leakage and a shop-shell that names its own slug-scoped containers. Delivery-currency caveat unchanged from lead-faua: reaches launched shops only after a shop-templates version bump+tag (separate maintenance the lead schedules).
close_reason: RED demonstrated (StepDefinitionNotFound, and dummyco-scoped assertions unproducible by pre-lead-faua hardcoded ops); feature + step defs committed test(red) f744dc3; 3 real pin-vs-impl gaps fixed feat(green) 1fc4cd8 (shop-shell slug-container grep, no ~/.claude//.gitconfig literals, compose no-fleet). notes:
mechanism_observation: agent-vault ops scripts authored-from-spec; ops file-set grew 4->6; delivery gated on a version bump — Three load-bearing notes for lead reconciliation of WS-2 (lead-w87b):

1. **AUTHORED FROM SPEC (not byte-ported).** The instance copies bin/agent-vault-provision / bin/agent-vault-check live in the shopsystem-product LEAD HOST instance, OUTSIDE this BC root — per ADR-018 D1/D2 the implementer cannot and did not read them. Both ops templates were authored from the behavioral contract in the dispatch (provision = human-gated Claude-OAuth dashboard paste + slug-derived broker/vault credential provisioning that STOPs for the dashboard step; check = non-fatal 30-day GitHub-PAT expiry advisory probing the slug-derived broker, exits 0 always). They satisfy the behavioral/structural acceptance pin but are NOT guaranteed byte-identical to the instance copies. If the lead wants verbatim fidelity it should reconcile the rendered template against the instance copy.

2. **OPS FILE-SET GREW 4 -> 6.** The rendered lead ops/ set went from {compose.yaml, shop-shell, Dockerfile.<slug>-shell, shop-scenario-completion} to additionally include {agent-vault-provision, agent-vault-check}. The LEAD-HELD ops file-SET scenario (the one enumerating which files bootstrap pours; the dispatch references 133/134/135/137/138 enumerating the prior files) should be RE-AUTHORED to enumerate the two new files so it stays pinned additively. BC-side coverage already updated: tests/test_ops_generification.py gained 6 slug-parametric assertions. No BC-pinned @scenario_hash in this repo's features/ asserts an EXACT ops file-set, so nothing in-BC broke; BC-pinned 135 (9bc85eced7685a40, Dockerfile) is byte-untouched and GREEN.

3. **DELIVERY CURRENCY.** This change reaches already-launched shops only AFTER a shop-templates version bump + tag (à la lead-nlff v0.4.0) — a SEPARATE maintenance step the lead must schedule; bootstrap/update of NEW shops picks it up immediately.

title: request_bugfix from lead (lead-faua): generify ops/ product-scoped (WS-2) + fold proven instance improvements
description: Generify rendered ops/ (compose+shop-shell+Dockerfile) so every shopsystem-* literal derives from the bootstrap product slug via the existing substitution mechanism; fold in pg_isready healthcheck, POSTGRES_PASSWORD default, agent-vault service, and broker-wired shop-shell (ADR-028 zero-host-coupling). Acceptance: two real bootstraps (dummyco-product -> zero shopsystem-* literals; shopsystem-product -> working additive). BC-pinned 135 stays green.
notes:
mechanism_observation: ops generification (lead-faua) supersedes/tightens 5 lead-held @scenario_hashes; re-author slug-parametric + bump — WS-2 made rendered ops/ product-scoped (every <product>-* literal derives from the bootstrap slug). The five LEAD-HELD conflicting hashes (not in this BC's features/) are now superseded/tightened and need re-authoring slug-parametric so the lead can keep them pinned. Per-hash impact:

- 721a626cd0146f86 (133, network literal 'shopsystem'): SUPERSEDED. compose network/container/volume are now <slug>. shopsystem-product default still renders network 'shopsystem' (additive), but the literal-'shopsystem' assertion should become slug-parametric.
- f39b2e176b954197 (138, pgdata from SHOPSYSTEM_DATA env-overridable, never-in-repo): TIGHTENED. data-env is now <SLUG>_DATA (DUMMYCO_DATA for dummyco). never-in-repo + env-overridable PRESERVED for every slug (default ${HOME}/.local/share/<slug>). Re-author env-var name slug-parametric.
- 4e99a6abbc57b884 (130, --shop-name canonical slug; name.md single source of truth): LOAD-BEARING, PRESERVED. This IS the slug source the generification reads (ops slug = --shop-name minus trailing -product). Not retired.
- bd4c9e3f3e391736 (134, shop-shell executable bash wrapper): TIGHTENED. body changed (broker-wiring, slug-derived container grep, error-if-no-token, agent-vault-check). PRESERVED: executable #!/usr/bin/env bash wrapper, 'docker compose ... up -d postgres', 'docker run', '--user vscode', no '--user $(id -u):$(id -g)'.
- f706707653aa646a (137, ops files shop-owned, NOT under .claude/canonical/): LOAD-BEARING, PRESERVED. Files still top-level / under bin/.

BC-pinned 9bc85eced7685a40 (135, Dockerfile recipe) UNCHANGED in this BC's features/ and stays GREEN (FROM/USER/CLI preserved; target filename now Dockerfile.<slug>-shell = shopsystem-shell for the shopsystem-product slug the scenario uses).

DESIGN DECISION (host-port): postgres HOST port defaults to deterministic 5432 + crc32(slug) % 1000, env-overridable via <SLUG>_POSTGRES_PORT, so a 2nd product never collides on the host port. Verified dummyco=5714, shopsystem=5829.

No invariant could-not-be-preserved.

title: request_bugfix from lead (lead-ajc9): place per-work_id worktrees under /workspace (.worktrees/<id>) not ../worktrees
description: Canonical using-git-worktrees skill places worktrees at ../worktrees/<id> (PARENT of /workspace, OUTSIDE the bind-mount), which trips a hard Claude-Code permission wall in-container even in bypass mode. Move to .worktrees/<id> inside the BC root; gitignore .worktrees/; fix load-bearing prose (L37/L94 'adjacent to') + add container rationale.
notes:
mechanism_observation: Canonical worktree-path fix reaches launched BCs only after a separate version-bump+tag delivery step — This work_id corrected the canonical `using-git-worktrees` SKILL and `gitignore.template` to place per-work_id worktrees at the in-root `.worktrees/<work_id>` (inside /workspace), on origin/main a217de7. Two load-bearing notes for the mechanism:

1. **Delivery currency caveat.** This is a canonical package-data change. Already-launched BCs vendored the old `../worktrees` skill at their own bootstrap/last-update time; they will only pick up the corrected doctrine after a shop-templates version bump + tag and a re-pour (the WS-4-style delivery step exercised previously for lead-slkk). That delivery is a separate work item NOT covered by lead-ajc9's acceptance pin (which scopes the canonical source edit + gitignore + rationale only). Until that bump ships, a freshly-bootstrapped BC gets the safe path but in-flight BCs still carry the old convention.

2. **Doctrine was lagging actual safe practice.** This BC's own router already created the lead-ajc9 worktree at /workspace/.worktrees/lead-ajc9 (in-/workspace), i.e. the corrected placement — so the canonical written doctrine had drifted behind the behavior the router was already enacting. The fix realigns the doctrine to the safe practice.
close_reason: Closed

title: implement D2 (inline doctrine, demote/drop §x across lead-architect/lead-po/bc-reviewer) and D3 (extract turn-limited exchange to PDR-014 skill)
notes:
mechanism_observation: sweep exceeded msg enumeration (lead-po §3.3, bc-reviewer §4.4×2); line nums drifted; delivery needs version bump+tag — Three load-bearing observations about the self-contained-templates contract surface (ADR-037 WS-4):

1. **Scope was BROADER than the message's line-by-line enumeration.** The dispatch enumerated lead-architect (6 sites) and lead-po §3.2 only. The criterion-based acceptance pin required sweeping ALL role templates, which surfaced TWO additional dangling-citation sites the enumeration omitted: lead-po **§3.3** (the 'artifacts collapse onto §3.3' line) and bc-reviewer **§4.4 ×2** (the Reviewer→lead clarify-loop refs). Both were dangling external citations identical in kind to the enumerated ones. The lead's contract-surface model should record that the resolution swept every role template (lead-architect, lead-po, bc-reviewer; bc-implementer was already clean), not just the enumerated lines — otherwise a future 'self-contained' audit re-derives the same misses.

2. **The message's enumerated line numbers had drifted.** They were captured from 'shop-templates show' on 2026-06-12 and were stale against the base (e.g. §3.4 turn-limited at message-L92 was actually ~L105; §6 at message-L266/346 vs actual ~L279/368). Enumerations keyed on line numbers rot between capture and dispatch; criterion-based pins (grep predicate, not line list) are the durable contract form and is what this work followed.

3. **Delivery currency is a SEPARATE step not in this work_id's acceptance.** The new PDR-014 skill (po-architect-decomposition-exchange) plus the template edits reach ALREADY-LAUNCHED lead instances only after a package version bump + tag (the lead-slkk pattern). This work_id's acceptance is the canonical change + its tests only; I did NOT bump the version or push a release tag. The lead must track the version-bump+tag delivery as a distinct WS-4 step so launched leads actually receive the self-contained templates and the new skill.

title: transform canonical lead.md to GREEN: reposition PRIME DIRECTIVE before SHOP_NAME heading (de-dup), replace choice-suppression body with 3-trigger positive reframe
notes:
GREEN: edit src/shop_templates/templates/claude/lead.md to the acceptance-pin end state
mechanism_observation: lead-f3gm DELTA EVIDENCE stale vs canonical lead.md; pins still genuinely unmet, reached idempotently — The request_bugfix DELTA EVIDENCE for lead-f3gm described a pre-state that did NOT match canonical templates/claude/lead.md on origin/main at dispatch (base 2872e61):

1. It claimed Block 1 (PRIME DIRECTIVE) was 'ENTIRELY ABSENT' and that 'shop-templates show lead' would show no opening block. In fact a verbatim-matching '# PRIME DIRECTIVE — act, do not ask' block ALREADY EXISTED — but MISPOSITIONED, after the '# {{SHOP_NAME}} — lead shop instructions' heading (~line 7), not opening the file.

2. It claimed Block 2 (choice-suppression) carried the 'OLDER, WEAKER prohibition-framed' body. In fact it was an INTERMEDIATE positive reframe (commit 2730ea0 had already partially reframed it): the body opened 'Per the **PRIME DIRECTIVE**, when a procedural choice arises...' with a 'Carve-out' framing and only TWO triggers (Scope or vocabulary; PO/Architect routing) — missing the third trigger, the DECIDE-AND-ACT lead, and the positive-standing-order closer.

The acceptance pins were NONETHELESS genuinely unmet, so the fix was REAL, not a no-op: pin 1 (position) was violated (block after the heading, not before) and pin 2 was violated (intermediate body lacked the 'DECIDE EVERY OPERATIONAL QUESTION YOURSELF AND ACT ON IT' lead, the third 'Unauthorized outward-facing action' trigger, and the closer). I reached the pins IDEMPOTENTLY by inspecting the actual file first and transforming to the end state (move+de-dup PD before heading; replace choice-suppression body), rather than blind-inserting per the stale pre-state.

Load-bearing for WS-4 part 2+: the lead's contract-surface model (its in-product .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md copy and 'shop-templates show lead' expectation) had drifted ahead of what it believed canonical carried. Future request_bugfix DELTA EVIDENCE for canonical lead.md should be recomputed against actual package data (read_claude_md_primer('lead')) at compose time, not against the lead's local primer copy, or the next dispatch may describe a pre-state that is already partly satisfied.
